text.title: Match Real World Worked Examples
text.summary: End-to-end worked examples: jargon rewrite, system-centric confirmation rewrite, and locale-aware date format.

example.title: Before
example.p: UI string `settings.email.smtp_label`: "Configure SMTP parameters for outbound mail relay".

example.title: Evidence
example.p: TKT-9182: "I just want my app to send emails — no idea what SMTP is".
INT-P14: "I gave up on the email step because I did not understand any of the labels".

example.title: Rewrite + consistency
example.p: Rewrite: "Set up email sending". Helper text: "Provide the server we should use to send emails on your behalf". consistency_check=needs-cross-surface-update — the same change applies in onboarding docs.

example.title: Before / After
example.p: Before: "Record created successfully." After: "Your booking is confirmed — confirmation #BK-201."
Evidence: INT-P22 — "I do not know what 'record' means here, did my booking go through or not?"

example.title: Before / After
example.p: Before: "Appointment 03/04/2025". After: "Appointment 4 Mar 2025" or "Appointment 2025-03-04" depending on locale.
Evidence: TKT-7711 (US user thought 4 Apr); TKT-7813 (EU user thought 3 Apr) — same string, opposite interpretations.
